element_introduction_order.mastery_context: New movement techniques for vertical and horizontal challenges
Progressive unlocking across worlds; powers unlock new level areas
Allows air navigation and block destruction
Projectile-based combat with unique positioning requirements
Directional momentum-based movement; difficult to control
Omnidirectional flight with charging attack system; most versatile power
Increased vertical mobility for platforming puzzles
Skill tests to acquire boss copy abilities
Culminating challenges guarding Star Rod pieces
Ultimate challenge after Star Rod reassembly
Optional life gain; teaches risk-reward through crane mechanics
Guaranteed ability access for puzzle completion

typical_relief_triggers: Consuming food items for health recovery
Accessing museums with guaranteed copy abilities
Warp Star doors enabling world backtracking
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy encounters with unknown copy ability patterns
Miniboss battles (teach new copy ability risks)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Graduated difficulty progression with alternating safe exploration (museums, lobbies) and dangerous skill tests (minibosses, bosses). Early worlds emphasize ability discovery through low-stakes enemy encounters; mid-worlds introduce demanding platforming challenges; late-worlds combine multiple abilities and tight timing. Minigame sections provide relief breaks within worlds.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Warp Star doors enable returning to previous worlds; arenas and museums are discretionary challenge rooms within lobby areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Vegetable Valley emphasizes basic inhalation, spitting, and movement mechanics in safe contexts. Copy abilities introduced gradually. Low health pressure, generous enemy spacing.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of challenging platforming with sliding, running, and early advanced abilities (Ball, Sword, Fireball). Miniboss encounters demand copy ability proficiency. Increased enemy density and hazard complexity.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Wheel and UFO abilities demand high mechanical precision and understanding of momentum systems. Boss encounters escalate difficulty significantly. Nightmare final boss combines all learned mechanics. Tight timing windows and consequence-heavy platforming.
notable_spikes: First miniboss arena encounter (introduces combat discipline)
Wheel ability introduction (pronounced difficulty spike due to momentum control)
UFO ability section (charging system adds complexity layer)
World 6-7 transitions (late-game minibosses and multi-ability platforming)
Final Nightmare boss (synthesizes all mechanics under extreme pressure)

notable_level_patterns.description: Early levels in each world gradually introduce new copy abilities through spaced enemy encounters. Museums provide guaranteed access to specific abilities before demanding their use in platforming sections.

Dedicated combat rooms containing minibosses (Poppy Bros. Sr., Mr. Frosty, Rolling Turtle, Mix). Defeating minibosses rewards health and access to boss's copy ability. Serves as skill checkpoint before world boss.
Climactic single-enemy encounters at world terminus. Boss patterns require mastery of recently introduced abilities. Defeat grants Star Rod piece and world progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Vegetable Valley boss through World 6 bosses

notable_level_patterns.description: Late-game platforming demanding precise control of Wheel and UFO ability momentum. Requires frame-perfect input or death/damage consequence. Creates tension cycles of high difficulty followed by safe lobby respite.

Safe central zone (World 1-7 lobbies) with 4-5 doors to regular levels, boss door, and Warp Star return option. Museums and minigames accessible within lobby areas. Enables optional content and backtracking.
Crane game and similar activities placed within worlds to provide mechanical break from platforming pressure. Optional life-gain mechanics reduce consequence pressure.
World 7 culminates in reassembly of Star Rod and ascent to Fountain of Dreams. Multi-stage confrontation with Nightmare boss combining visual spectacle with mechanically synthesized challenges.
